100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 5, 1926, the British Empire faced growing unrest as labor strikes intensified, particularly in coal mining regions, raising concerns over economic stability. In the United States, the Senate continued its deliberations on the McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at supporting farmers through federal price supports, provoking debates on agricultural policy. Meanwhile, in Italy, Mussolini's government tightened its grip on power, further suppressing dissent in a move that signaled the age of fascism.
Updated: Thursday, March 5, 2026 at 06:29:19 AM CST
